channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump administration says it cut funding to some life-saving UN food programs by mistake
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The State Department said Tuesday that it had rolled back an undisclosed number of sweeping funding cuts to U.N. World Food Program emergency projects in 14 impoverished countries, saying it had terminated some of the contracts for life-saving aid by mistake.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mike Huckabee nomination advances in Senate, one step closer to becoming ambassador to Israel
item.description: The Senate moved closer to confirming Mike Huckabee as ambassador to Israel amid backlash over his past remarks on Palestinians and Trump’s vision for the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: National Park Service restores original Harriet Tubman, Underground Railroad webpage
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The National Park Service has reversed edits and restored content to its webpage about Harriet Tubman and the Underground Railroad in the wake of news reports and public backlash over the changes.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court blocks order requiring Trump administration to reinstate thousands of federal workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Tuesday blocked an order for the Trump administration to return to work thousands of federal employees who were let go in mass firings aimed at dramatically downsizing the federal government.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dem accuses Trump admin of 'fire sale approach' as GOP aims to lower federal gov's real estate portfolio
item.description: Democrats accused the Trump administration of "looting" the federal government during a Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) subcommittee hearing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Republicans and Democrats say the US must maintain its troop totals in Europe
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican and Democratic members of the House Armed Services Committee voiced sharp bipartisan criticism Tuesday about reports the Trump administration is eyeing a reduction of U.S. forces in Europe, saying America must stick with its NATO allies. Rep. Michael Rogers, R-Mich.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Democrat says he'd vote to protect Trump's tariff plan, worries White House will stand down
item.description: Rep. Jared Golden, D-Maine, said he stands with President Trump's tariff plan and would vote to protect the economic measure if it were brought for a vote.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Future uncertain for Duke basketball star due to new Trump policy
item.description: A new visa policy announced by the Trump administration last weekend could impact South Sudanese passport holders, including Duke basketball phenom Khaman Maluach.
item.dc:creator: Keaton Eberly, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jeffries claims Trump budget bill will steal food from 'the mouths of babies' while enriching billionaires
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries claimed a Trump-backed budget bill would take food from the mouths of babies will enriching billionaires.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New audit, renewed criticism of Met Council's oversight of Southwest light rail project
item.description: The release of a new audit of the Southwest Corridor light rail project in the Twin Cities prompted sharp questions and bipartisan criticism from Minnesota lawmakers on Monday. Officials with the Metropolitan Council said the line is on track to begin service in 2027.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Boris Johnson pecked by ostrich
item.description: Former British Prime Minister Boris Johnson had a fowl encounter with an ostrich during a sight-seeing trip in Texas, with video showing him getting nipped by the bird while his son laughed in his lap.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Spanberger raises $6.7 million in Virginia governor race
item.description: Former Rep. Abigail Spanberger’s (D-Va.) gubernatorial campaign said Monday it raised $6.7 million in the first quarter of the year. The haul included more than 50,000 donations of $100 or less in the first three months of the year, according to the Spanberger campaign.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Live updates: Trump budget blueprint opponents dig in, China tariff war escalates
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is racing to get House Republicans behind a Senate-passed framework for enacting large swaths of President Trump's domestic agenda, even as conservative opposition to the measure grows.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Trump expected to sign executive orders to boost coal
item.description: President Donald Trump is expected to sign executive orders Tuesday aimed at boosting coal, a reliable but polluting energy source that’s long been in decline.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Florida bill ignites debate over parental consent for minors' healthcare
item.description: A parental rights bill moving through the state legislature is sparking heated debate over access to reproductive healthcare for minors and whether or not parents should have a say.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Stock market tumble impacts retirement savings; Florida economists explain
item.description: “This panic, or phase, is not going to last more than 30 days. The stock market, sooner than later, will bounce back,” said Tampa Economist Federico Alves.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump approval slips to lowest point in second term: Survey
item.description: President Trump’s approval rating slipped to its lowest point during his second White House term amid his handling of the economy and the recent Houthi Signal chat leak, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Here's Gov. Joe Lombardo's plan to solve the Nevada housing crisis
item.description: A $250 million plan to spur construction of homes and apartments for low- to middle-income Nevadans is projected to provide homes for 16,000 people, according to officials who presented it Wednesday night in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here's how newly imposed tariffs will affect California products
item.description: President Donald Trump’s widespread tariffs could have a profound impact on California’s exports, according to a study from the Public Policy Institute of California.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada Democrats outnumber Republicans by just 2 voters amid statewide switch to nonpartisan
item.description: After Nevada Republicans surpassed their Democratic counterparts in voter registration for the first time in nearly 20 years, Democrats have once again taken over as the state's top party -- with a voter registration lead of two.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California lawmakers reject bills to ban trans athletes' participation in girls sports
item.description: California lawmakers won't change state policies allowing transgender kids and teens to play on sports teams consistent with their gender identities.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, Fox 5
